address: Pa O Don Chai Road, A. Muang, Chiang Rai

description.en:
## Overview

Wat Rong Khun, universally known as the White Temple, is the most photographed landmark in Chiang Rai and one of Thailand's most recognizable modern attractions. Located about 13 kilometers south of Chiang Rai city on the highway toward Chiang Mai, this dazzling white structure was designed by Thai visual artist Chalermchai Kositpipat, who began construction in 1997 and continues to expand the complex with his own funds. Unlike traditional Thai temples, the White Temple is a contemporary art installation that uses Buddhist symbolism in unconventional and sometimes provocative ways.

## Architecture and Art

The main ubosot is covered entirely in white plaster and thousands of mirrored glass fragments that glitter brilliantly under sunlight, symbolizing the purity of the Buddha. The approach crosses the Bridge of Rebirth over a field of outstretched hands representing desire and greed, a dramatic installation that sets the tone for the entire visit. Inside the main hall, traditional Buddhist murals share wall space with modern pop-culture imagery including superheroes and movie characters, all woven into themes of temptation, suffering, and enlightenment. Surrounding the main building, the grounds feature elaborate sculptures, fountains, and ornamental gardens that take two to three hours to fully explore.

## The Golden Building and Art Gallery

The striking golden building near the entrance houses the temple's restrooms and is meant to represent worldly desire, creating a deliberate contrast with the pure white of the main hall. The Cave of Art gallery contains paintings and artwork by Chalermchai and is now included in the admission ticket. The artist's stated goal is to build the most beautiful temple in the world and dedicate it as an offering to Lord Buddha, with plans for nine buildings in total when the complex is eventually completed.

## Practical Information

The White Temple is open daily from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. As of January 2026, the entrance fee for foreign visitors is 200 THB, which was doubled from the previous 100 THB and now includes access to the Cave of Art. Thai nationals enter free of charge. Children under 120 centimeters tall and visitors aged 70 and above also receive free admission. The best time to visit is early morning right at opening to avoid the tour bus crowds that arrive from Chiang Mai around 10:00 AM. Photography is not allowed inside the main hall but is permitted throughout the rest of the grounds.
